paper_title: Order Splitting and Liquidity Replenishment Are Jointly Necessary for the Square-Root Law of Market Impact: A Counterfactual Dissection
human_review_reasons: architecture_plan.json flags a High-severity risk: HFT adaptive-spread rule is not numerically specified in the paper

Stage 6 comparison (2026-07-11) found Critical deviations on both reported metrics (baseline delta +90.3%, no_splitting delta +212.9%) and recommended manual review before further automated comparison

Post-comparison debugging (outside the formal 6-stage pipeline) identified and patched a flat HFT quote-depth profile in src/sqrt_law_abm/models/agents.py; fix is unit-tested and smoke-tested but not yet confirmed against a full-scale user re-run

retry_history.reason: User's iterative testing (60k steps -> 200k steps -> tuned HFT spread config) isolated that baseline delta improved to ~0.542 after config tuning, but no_splitting delta moved the wrong direction (0.977 -> 0.843 vs paper's 0.324). Root-caused to HFTMarketMakerAgent posting flat quote size across all 50 levels, giving a linear (gamma=0) depth profile instead of the paper's reported concentrated-near-best-quote profile (gamma in [1.5, 3.6]).

notes: pipeline_complete=true reflects that all 6 formal stages have run at least once. However, the Stage 6 comparison on file is now STALE: it reflects the pre-patch code and the user's earliest (60k-step) results. A fresh Stage 6 comparison is recommended once the user re-runs with the patched agents.py and reports new baseline/no_splitting numbers -- see retry_history above.
